content: Global health burden measurement quantifies population health losses from diseases, injuries, and risk factors to inform policy priorities. Key metrics: Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) combine years of life lost (YLL) from premature mortality and years lived with disability (YLD) weighted by severity; Quality-Adjusted Life Years (QALYs) measure health gains for economic evaluation; Healthy Life Expectancy (HALE) estimates years expected in good health. Global Burden of Disease (GBD) Study provides comprehensive assessments across 204 countries, 371 diseases/injuries, 88 risk factors. Epidemiological transition describes shift from infectious to chronic diseases as populations develop, though many countries face double burden. Leading global DALY causes 2021: ischemic heart disease, stroke, COVID-19, lower respiratory infections, neonatal disorders. Substantial variation by region—Sub-Saharan Africa still dominated by infectious diseases, high-income countries by chronic conditions. Risk factor attribution identifies prevention opportunities.
